chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: In today's video, I will tell you about CSS transforms.  There are two types of transforms on CSS.  One is 2D transform and the other is 3D transform.  2D transform means we want to change any object in two-dimensional space.  Like we can increase its scale, we can translate it, we can rotate it.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: 3D transform on the other hand works on three dimensions.  That is, we have an additional axis, z-axis.  Because of which we can manipulate an object in three dimensions.  Don't get confused about how these things are done.  Just like the video and write Sigma Batch OP in the comment section below.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And I will explain everything else in this video.  Let's go to the computer screen.  Let's roll the intro.  Intro  So guys, I have come to my computer screen.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And here I have already prepared for you.  And the thing I am going to show you here is going to be fun for you too.  So first of all, we will take a very basic start.  I will make an index.html here.  I will put a template on the boiler.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: There is a very basic thing, CSS transform.  So I will tell you about it.  So let's say I have a div, which has a container.  There is a box inside it.  I will give some styles to the box.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And first of all, I will give it a height.  I will give it a width of 50 pixels.  I will give it a background color of 50 pixels.  I will give it a border of 2 pixels.  Solid black.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Let's see its preview.  And its preview looks like this.  Now let's say that our box is inside the container.  So I will give the container a width of 100 pixels.  I will give it a height of 80 pixels.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And I will give it a border of the same color.  I will change the border color of the bus.  Purple.  So here in the box of the border of purple color,  I have got this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Very good.  Now let's see what the transform property does.  The transform property applies some transforms in the element.  The first transform I want to show you is rotate.  Let's say I want to rotate the box.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So I will put a transform property here.  I will say transform.  And I will say rotate.  Now in rotate, you can see that it is rotate, rotate 3D, rotate x, rotate y, rotate z.  First of all, I show you rotate.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Let's say I want to rotate it by 40 degrees.  So it will rotate by 40 degrees.  I will do one more thing.  I will write a box in it.  So that you can see the box here.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And if you want things to be centered in it,  you know what to do.  Display flex, justify content center,  online item center.  Now you can see that the box will come in the center.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And I have rotated it.  If I want, I can do it by 45 degrees.  I can do it by 40 degrees.  That's my choice.  But there is another unit here, which is famous, which is turn.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: I say 0.25 turn.  If 360 degrees is one turn,  then I want to rotate it by 0.25 turn.  So it has rotated by 90 degrees.  And here you can see that it has turned by 90 degrees.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: If I turn it by 0.5 turn,  then you know that it will rotate by 180 degrees.  And one turn means  that it will rotate by 180 degrees.  That is, 360 degree rotation.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Then here we can rotate it around an axis.  Like I can say here transform.  And I can write transform here.  Rotate.  Suppose I want to rotate it.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Rotate x.  So it will rotate around the x-axis.  And suppose I write here 40 degrees.  I can write 40 deg here.  And I will write it like this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And you see that  it will rotate by 40 degrees in x.  I have not put a semicolon.  Now you see that it has rotated by 40 degrees in x.  Now when I say it has rotated in x,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: it has rotated around the x-axis.  That is, if this is my x-axis,  and this is my y-axis.  And z-axis is inside this screen.  So it is z-axis.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Now here you can think that  this is my box.  Which was kept like this.  I will show you on full screen.  If this is my box like this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And this is my x-axis.  So it will rotate around the x-axis.  Or it will rotate like this.  So according to that,  if I give it a 90 degree rotation around the x-axis,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: then it will become paper thin.  And you will not see it.  And if I do rotate y here,  then it will rotate like this.  And if I want, I can rotate it just 90 degrees.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Now you see that it will rotate a little bit.  Around the y-axis.  And if you rotate around the z-axis,  then it will also rotate.  You can also rotate z.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: It will rotate around the z-axis.  Such that your axis is going inside the screen.  So see it will rotate on this plane.  Because the axis is going inside the screen.  I hope you understood this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So this is our rotate property.  After this comes the scale property.  You can also make it bigger or smaller in scale.  For example, if I remove it.  And I write here transform.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And I write here scale.  So see we have a lot of options in scale.  And I have one option.  1.5 or 1.6 times scale.  So see it has increased by 1.6 times.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And if I want it to be bigger in x,  then I can scale x.  If I want it to be bigger in y,  then I can scale y.  And in the same way,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: our other scale properties work.  You just have to write scale.  And you can do it in scale 3D.  When you do scale 3D,  then a 3D transformation is applied.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And now I will tell you about skew.  And if I assume skew,  I will do it, let us say 5 degrees.  So I will show you by semicolon.  And as soon as I skew it from 5 degrees,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: it will be skewed.  Skew means, suppose someone pulled it from here and here.  Put a force like this and put a force like this.  So it will be skewed.  You can also do skew in y-axis.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: You can write y-axis, you can do 34 degrees.  But there is no skew in z-axis.  So you have to remember this thing that you cannot do skew in z-axis.  Okay.  So skew is not applied for z-axis at all.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So I will remove skew from here.  And I will show you that you can also translate.  Translate is the most used property.  I am telling you from my experience.  Suppose I say 34 pixels.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So see, it will be translated from 34 pixels.  Okay.  If I say 34%, then its width  will be translated from 34%.  If I say 100%,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: then it will be translated from 100%.  And I can also translate it in x.  And I can also translate it in y.  Now see here, if I translate it in y,  then it will come down.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Now sometimes what happens is that we have to apply  more than one transform properties.  And when we have to apply more than one  transform properties, then what we will do?  Then we can apply it simply from spaces.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Suppose I want to skew it too.  I want to skew x. Let us say  40 degrees.  And I also want to translate x.  And I will say translate x.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And I will say it is safe 45%.  So all three transform properties  have been applied at the same time.  See, it has been translated. It has been skewed in x and y.  Whatever I will put here, it will be done.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Okay. I hope you understood  how you can apply multiple transform properties.  Now when is the use of transform  done? Mainly it is done  when we are making a navbar from here.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: And I want the navbar to be hidden.  So I can translate it to minus 100 pixels.  I can do minus 100%.  So it will come out from here.  See, it has come out of y.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So we can do something like this.  If I do star here in this way,  I say star and I say m0  and I say p0 here.  And by putting margin padding 0,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: I have done this.  So it has gone up.  So we can do something like this.  I did minus 100% in translate y.  If I do minus 45% in translate x,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: then it will go in the negative direction.  Where is my minus button of the keyboard?  I have pressed it. See, it has gone in the x-axis.  So you can do something like this.  Now there is another property, transform origin.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: What is this? Now I will tell you what it is.  Suppose I rotate it by 0.5 turns.  From where and about will it rotate?  I will make it 0.25  so that it turns only from 45 degrees.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Sorry, 0.125 will have to be done  for 45 degrees. So see, it is turning  from 45 degrees. And  if I show you that the rotation  point is its center. So basically

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: my box is here. The default property  of transform origin is 50-50.  It is the center. Now suppose  I make it 0-0. I say that  this is my 0 and this is also my 0.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So this rotation will be about this point.  I will show you. I will change it  to degrees. Suppose I make it  55 degrees.  So as soon as I increase this number,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: you see the rotation is about this point.  About 0-0 which is the origin.  Now as soon as this value increases,  which is 0%, suppose I make it 10%.  Our origin is increasing.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Now our rotation will be about this point.  As you can see.  So our rotation will be  about this point which you  are seeing on your screen.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: This point in this box  means if I don't put a transform  in it,  then from here 10% and  from here, suppose I write

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: 50%, then at the halfway point  the rotation point will come.  Wherever its coordinates will come,  it will rotate about that point.  I hope you understood this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: It is used when you want to rotate  about a particular point. But it is an advanced concept.  It is used less.  I will tell you this too. After this, I want to show you  a special thing.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: I hope you understood all these things.  I have made an exhaustive list of demos.  In this, I have put a style.css.  Let's preview it.  You will know. See here,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: I have tried to cover all  our transforms.  See here, scale x  scales like this.  Scale y scales like this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Scale will do something like this in both x and y.  Translate x will do like this.  Translate y will do like this.  Translate will do like this. If there is a value in both x and y,  and you want to see which value

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: is there, then you can see from here.  I have not told you the transition yet.  I will tell you that. So you  wait for the transition.  Matrix transformation is this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Rotate is this. Skew is this.  Skew x is this. Skew y is this.  Matrix 3D is this. Translate 3D  is this. Rotate will be like this.  Which axis is about to rotate?

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Y axis. See here,  it rotates about x axis.  When we rotated it at 90 degrees,  it disappeared.  Translate z will do like this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: It will seem to go down.  I have translated it in both x and y.  So that you can see that it is entering inside the screen.  But you can see what is going on here.  Now our monitor

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: is a 2D screen.  But these transform properties  make it seem that  our rotation is happening in 3D.  So the CSS is made like this.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: Such that  you understood the demo well.  I have put together the demo in such a way  that you can see in the comment and find out  what is going on here.

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: So I hope this demo will be helpful for you.  Try these properties yourself.  I hope you will enjoy  after seeing all this.  If you have not accessed the Sigma web development playlist,

chunks.title: CSS Transforms MasterClass
chunks.text: then access it now.  Either click here to bookmark it  or click here to save it.  Source code is being updated on GitHub.  Thank you so much guys for watching this video.
